query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.174 (commit: 6d2f8951). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Answer comparison not shown when type answer into the card is enabled ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? - Enable `Advanced → Type answer into the card` - Review a card that requires typing the answer. ### Expected behaviour After clicking "Show Answer", the comparision of the typed and actual answers should be shown. It is the case when the above-mentioned Advanced option is disabled. ### Actual behaviour Only the correct answer is shown. ### Debug info ```text 2.17 beta 4 ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? This might be a long-standing bug. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/scripts/card.js` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/TypeAnswer.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→/* 2→ * Copyright (c) 2021 David Allison 3→ * 4→ * This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under 5→ * the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software 6→ * Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later 7→ * version. 8→ * 9→ * This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY 10→ * WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A 11→ * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details. 12→ * 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with 14→ * this program. If not, see . 15→ */ 16→ 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer 18→ 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences 20→import android.content.res.Resources 21→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting 22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager 23→import com.ichi2.anki.R 24→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.LanguageHint 25→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.LanguageHintService 26→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card 27→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection 28→import com.ichi2.utils.jsonObjectIterable 29→import org.intellij.lang.annotations.Language 30→import org.json.JSONArray 31→import timber.log.Timber 32→import java.util.regex.Matcher 33→import java.util.regex.Pattern 34→ 35→// TODO: investigate whether it's feasible to drop the useInputTag option introduced back 36→// in 2015. https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/3921 37→ 38→class TypeAnswer( 39→ @get:JvmName("useInputTag") val useInputTag: Boolean, 40→ @get:JvmName("doNotUseCodeFormatting") val doNotUseCodeFormatting: Boolean, 41→ /** Preference: Whether the user wants to focus "type in answer" */ 42→ val autoFocus: Boolean 43→) { 44→ 45→ /** The correct answer in the compare to field if answer should be given by learner. Null if no answer is expected. */ 46→ var correct: String? = null 47→ private set 48→ 49→ /** What the learner actually typed (externally mutable) */ 50→ var input = "" 51→ 52→ /** Font face of the 'compare to' field */ 53→ var font = "" 54→ private set 55→ 56→ /** The font size of the 'compare to' field */ 57→ var size = 0 58→ private set 59→ 60→ var languageHint: LanguageHint? = null 61→ private set 62→ 63→ /** 64→ * Optional warning for when a typed answer can't be displayed 65→ * 66→ * * empty card [R.string.empty_card_warning] 67→ * * unknown field specified [R.string.unknown_type_field_warning] 68→ * */ 69→ var warning: String? = null 70→ private set 71→ 72→ /** 73→ * @return true If entering input via EditText 74→ * and if the current card has a {{type:field}} on the card template 75→ */ 76→ fun validForEditText(): Boolean { 77→ return !useInputTag && correct != null 78→ } 79→ 80→ fun autoFocusEditText(): Boolean { 81→ return validForEditText() && autoFocus 82→ } 83→ 84→ /** 85→ * Extract type answer/cloze text and font/size 86→ * @param card The next card to display 87→ */ 88→ fun updateInfo(col: Collection, card: Card, res: Resources) { 89→ correct = null 90→ val q = card.question(col) 91→ val m = PATTERN.matcher(q) 92→ var clozeIdx = 0 93→ if (!m.find()) { 94→ return 95→ } 96→ var fldTag = m.group(1)!! 97→ // if it's a cloze, extract data 98→ if (fldTag.startsWith("cloze:")) { 99→ // get field and cloze position 100→ clozeIdx = card.ord + 1 101→ fldTag = fldTag.split(":").toTypedArray()[1] 102→ } 103→ // loop through fields for a match 104→ val flds: JSONArray = card.model(col).getJSONArray("flds") 105→ for (fld in flds.jsonObjectIterable()) { 106→ val name = fld.getString("name") 107→ if (name == fldTag) { 108→ correct = card.note(col).getItem(name) 109→ if (clozeIdx != 0) { 110→ // narrow to cloze 111→ correct = contentForCloze(correct!!, clozeIdx) 112→ } 113→ font = fld.getString("font") 114→ size = fld.getInt("size") 115→ languageHint = LanguageHintService.getLanguageHintForField(fld) 116→ break 117→ } 118→ } 119→ when (correct) { 120→ null -> { 121→ warning = if (clozeIdx != 0) { 122→ res.getString(R.string.empty_card_warning) 123→ } else { 124→ res.getString(R.string.unknown_type_field_warning, fldTag) 125→ } 126→ } 127→ "" -> { 128→ correct = null 129→ } 130→ else -> { 131→ warning = null 132→ } 133→ } 134→ } 135→ 136→ /** 137→ * Format question field when it contains typeAnswer or clozes. If there was an error during type text extraction, a 138→ * warning is displayed 139→ * 140→ * @param buf The question text 141→ * @return The formatted question text 142→ */ 143→ fun filterQuestion(buf: String): String { 144→ val m = PATTERN.matcher(buf) 145→ if (warning != null) { 146→ return m.replaceFirst(warning!!) 147→ } 148→ val sb = java.lang.StringBuilder() 149→ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 150→ if (useInputTag) { 151→ // These functions are defined in the JavaScript file assets/scripts/card.js. We get the text back in 152→ // shouldOverrideUrlLoading() in createWebView() in this file. 153→ append( 154→ """
155→ 0) { 160→ append("style=\"font-family: '").append(font).append("'; font-size: ") 161→ .append(size).append("px;\" ") 162→ } 163→ append(">\n
\n") 164→ } else { 165→ append("........") 167→ } 168→ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 169→ } 170→ 171→ /** 172→ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison: `[[type:(.+?)]]` 173→ * 174→ * Replaces with the HTML for the correct answer, and the comparison to the correct answer if appropriate. 175→ * 176→ * @param answer The card content on the back of the card 177→ * 178→ * @return The formatted answer text with `[[type:(.+?)]]` replaced with HTML 179→ */ 180→ fun filterAnswer(answer: String): String { 181→ val userAnswer = input 182→ val correctAnswer = correct 183→ Timber.d("correct answer = %s", correctAnswer) 184→ Timber.d("user answer = %s", userAnswer) 185→ return filterAnswer(answer, userAnswer, correctAnswer ?: "") 186→ } 187→ 188→ /** 189→ * Fill the placeholder for the type comparison. Show the correct answer, and the comparison if appropriate. 190→ * 191→ * @param answer The answer text 192→ * @param userAnswer Text typed by the user, or empty. 193→ * @param correctAnswer The correct answer, taken from the note. 194→ * @return The formatted answer text 195→ */ 196→ fun filterAnswer(answer: String, userAnswer: String, correctAnswer: String): String { 197→ val m: Matcher = PATTERN.matcher(answer) 198→ val sb = StringBuilder() 199→ fun append(@Language("HTML") html: String) = sb.append(html) 200→ 201→ var comparisonText = CollectionManager.compareAnswer(correctAnswer, userAnswer) 202→ if (doNotUseCodeFormatting) { 203→ comparisonText = "$comparisonText" 204→ } 205→ append(Matcher.quoteReplacement(comparisonText)) 206→ return m.replaceAll(sb.toString()) 207→ } 208→ 209→ companion object { 210→ /** Regular expression in card data for a 'type answer' after processing has occurred */ 211→ val PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[\\[type:(.+?)]]") 212→ 213→ fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences): TypeAnswer { 214→ return TypeAnswer( 215→ useInputTag = preferences.getBoolean("useInputTag", false), 216→ doNotUseCodeFormatting = preferences.getBoolean("noCodeFormatting", false), 217→ autoFocus = preferences.getBoolean("autoFocusTypeInAnswer", true) 218→ ) 219→ } 220→ 221→ /** 222→ * Return the correct answer to use for {{type::cloze::NN}} fields. 223→ * 224→ * @param txt The field text with the clozes 225→ * @param idx The index of the cloze to use 226→ * @return If the cloze strings are the same, return a single cloze string, otherwise, return 227→ * a string with a comma-separeted list of strings with the correct index. 228→ */ 229→ @VisibleForTesting 230→ fun contentForCloze(txt: String, idx: Int): String? { 231→ // In Android, } should be escaped 232→ val re = Pattern.compile("\\{\\{c$idx::(.+?)\\}\\}") 233→ val m = re.matcher(txt) 234→ val matches: MutableList = ArrayList() 235→ var groupOne: String 236→ while (m.find()) { 237→ groupOne = m.group(1)!! 238→ val colonColonIndex = groupOne.indexOf("::") 239→ if (colonColonIndex > -1) { 240→ // Cut out the hint. 241→ groupOne = groupOne.substring(0, colonColonIndex) 242→ } 243→ matches.add(groupOne) 244→ } 245→ val uniqMatches: Set = HashSet(matches) // Allow to check whether there are distinct strings 246→ 247→ // Make it consistent with the Desktop version (see issue #8229) 248→ return if (uniqMatches.size == 1) { 249→ matches[0] 250→ } else { 251→ matches.joinToString(", ") 252→ } 253→ } 254→ } 255→} 256→
